spiritual-cramp.d: San Francisco punks with Clash-style swagger; the razor-sharp first pit of the weekend.
spiritual-cramp.bio: San Francisco punks blending '77 snot, mod swagger and dub bounce. Sophomore LP 'Rude' (Oct 2025, prod. John Congleton) features a Sharon Van Etten duet — live they're a nonstop, amp-hopping party.

news.t: KQED: SF punks poised to blow up with 'Rude'
AltPress goes behind the making of 'Rude'

vince-staples.d: Long Beach rap's sharpest wit; deadpan bars over sleek West Coast bounce.
vince-staples.bio: Long Beach's sharpest wit dropped 'Cry Baby' (2026), his first independent album via Loma Vista, after wrapping two acclaimed seasons of Netflix's 'The Vince Staples Show.' Deadpan banter, airtight West Coast sets.

news.t: 'The Vince Staples Show' S2 trailer and release date

four-tet.d: Kieran Hebden's blissed-out, genre-melting electronica; a master of the festival build.
four-tet.bio: Kieran Hebden's beloved electronic project, blending pastoral melody with club weight. His 2026 run hits Bonnaroo amid huge headline shows, with hypnotic light-rig visuals and sets that drift from ambient to rave.

news.t: Four Tet adds 2026 dates incl. Brooklyn Storehouse

skrillex.d: Bass-music titan headlining Thursday; expect a relentless, surprise-stuffed rave.
skrillex.bio: Sonny Moore opens the festival Thursday — his first Bonnaroo since 2014's Mothership era — riding the Grammy-nominated F*CK U Skrillex album; expect a genre-shredding, guest-stacked main-stage assault.

news.t: Skrillex returns to Bonnaroo after 12 years
Skrillex, Strokes, Noah Kahan headline Bonnaroo

villanelle.d: Gene Gallagher (Liam's son) fronts this London trio reviving 90s grunge snarl.
villanelle.bio: London grunge trio fronted by Gene Gallagher — yes, Liam's son — channeling Alice In Chains and Soundgarden over Britpop swagger. Debut EP 'Measly Means' (2026) backs up the famous name with real volume.

news.t: NME Radar: Villanelle forge their own grungy image
Rolling Stone on Villanelle's nineties-grunge revival

amble.d: Irish folk trio whose hushed three-part harmonies became a word-of-mouth phenomenon.
amble.bio: Irish folk trio whose 2025 debut 'Reverie' spent four weeks at #1 in Ireland; they sold 130k+ headline tickets last year and opened for Hozier at Fenway. Hushed three-part harmonies that silence a tent.

news.t: Amble: 'We are just being who we are'
Debut album 'Reverie' revealed

blues-traveler.d: John Popper's harmonica-led 90s jam vets; 'Run-Around' at golden hour.
blues-traveler.bio: John Popper's harmonica heroes marked 30 years of 'Run-Around' in 2025 and hit Bonnaroo amid a packed 2026 slate with Gin Blossoms and their annual July 4th Red Rocks bash. Jam-band comfort food, played hot.

news.t: Blues Traveler expand 2026 tour incl. Bonnaroo
Co-headlining 2026 run with Gin Blossoms

yungblud.d: UK rock firebrand with arena-size hooks and chaotic, crowd-hugging energy.
yungblud.bio: Doncaster's rock evangelist rode 2025's 'Idols' to three Grammy noms and won Best Rock Performance for 'Changes' at Ozzy Osbourne's final show — dedicating it to Ozzy. 'Idols II' is on the way; expect total chaos.

news.t: Rolling Stone: Yungblud on 'Idols' II and honoring Ozzy
Yungblud's 'Changes' wins Best Rock Performance Grammy

griz.d: Sax-wielding electro-soul party-starter; pure feel-good funk and bass.
griz.bio: Funk-bass producer and saxophonist back from a two-year hiatus with 2025's GEMiNI EP, Future Funk Volume 1, and his own Seven Stars festival. Expect live sax over filthy drops and pure rainbow-colored joy.

news.t: GRiZ on his hiatus: 'I needed to figure out who I am'
GRiZ drops dazzling album Future Funk Volume 1

the-strokes.d: NYC garage-rock icons headlining Friday with era-defining anthems.
the-strokes.bio: NYC icons in a genuine new era: seventh LP 'Reality Awaits' (Rick Rubin, due July 2026) is their first since 2020, previewed by 'Going Shopping.' Rare US festival dates — with Valensi on a temporary live break.

news.t: NME: 'Reality Awaits' delayed, huge NYC show announced
The Strokes map 2026 world tour around new album

lambrini-girls.d: Brighton's confrontational queer-punk duo; the rowdiest, funniest pit on Friday.
lambrini-girls.bio: Brighton noise-punk duo whose 2025 debut Who Let The Dogs Out skewers misogyny, gentrification and nepo babies in 29 furious minutes. Live shows are confrontational, hilarious and famously pit-heavy.

news.t: Kerrang reviews Who Let The Dogs Out
Who Let the Dogs Out: righteous noise done right

wolfmother.d: Aussie riff-lords; 'Joker and the Thief' is pure Sabbath-sized catharsis live.
wolfmother.bio: Andrew Stockdale's Aussie retro-rockers are playing their five-times-platinum 2005 debut front to back on a 20th-anniversary 2026 tour — 'Woman,' 'Joker & the Thief' and 'Dimension' guaranteed.

news.t: Wolfmother to play debut album in full on 2026 tour

zack-fox.d: Comedian-rapper menace; equal parts standup, trolling, and genuine bangers.
zack-fox.bio: Atlanta comedian-rapper-DJ ('Jesus Is the One') whose UWAY party series toured North America into 2026, plus an acclaimed turn in Sundance thriller 'Lurker.' Expect jokes woven between house, jungle and rap edits.

news.t: RA: Zack Fox details 2025 North American tour

wet-leg.d: Isle of Wight deadpan indie stars; 'Chaise Longue' to 'catch these fists', all teeth.
wet-leg.bio: Isle of Wight pranksters gone full rock band: 'Moisturizer' (July 2025) topped the UK chart and bagged three Grammy noms. Rhian Teasdale leads a louder, sweatier live show than the 'Chaise Longue' days.

news.t: Treble reviews Wet Leg's bolder 'Moisturizer'
The Dare remixes 'mangetout' for the dancefloor

jessie-murph.d: Alabama's smoky-voiced country-pop disruptor, riding 'Blue Strips' to superstardom.
jessie-murph.bio: Alabama-born genre-bender whose 2025 album Sex Hysteria (with Gucci Mane and Lil Baby) hit the Billboard top 10. Her smoky country-trap-pop voice and theatrical Hysteria-era staging translate big live.

news.t: Cover story: Sex Hysteria is an open letter

mt-joy.d: Philly indie-folk warmth; sunset singalongs built for the Which Stage.
mt-joy.bio: Philly indie-folk rockers touring behind fourth LP Hope We Have Fun (May 2025), a Top 10 streamer with the Maren Morris duet 'Highway Queen'; they've graduated to arenas and MSG-sized singalongs.

news.t: Matt Quinn talks 'Hope We Have Fun' on NPR
Mt. Joy announce Hope We Have Fun Tour: Part 2

turnstile.d: Baltimore hardcore turned ecstatic stage-dive communion; an unmissable late-night set.
turnstile.bio: Baltimore's genre-melting hardcore heroes made history with five Grammy noms for 'Never Enough' (2025), winning Best Rock Album and Best Metal Performance. Their sets are pure communal mosh euphoria.

news.t: Rolling Stone: Turnstile on historic Grammy noms
Turnstile win big at the 2026 Grammys

pawpaw-rod.d: Smooth-talking soul-funk rapper; lowrider grooves for an easy afternoon.
pawpaw-rod.bio: Funk-soul charmer who parlayed four EPs and 200M+ streams into Picture Day, his long-awaited debut album; expect breezy, feel-good grooves delivered with festival-honed swagger.

news.t: PawPaw Rod curates a playlist for NME's The Cover
Picture Day Q&A: PawPaw Rod's debut album

goldie-boutilier.d: Nova Scotia chanteuse making cinematic femme-fatale pop.
goldie-boutilier.bio: Canadian singer-songwriter praised by Elton John, performing as her Scarface-inspired alter ego from 2025's Goldie Montana — cinematic, noir-glam pop she calls being 'a gangster in a gown.'

news.t: Goldie Boutilier steps into her prime as Goldie Montana
Goldie talks new single and alter ego at ACL

rachel-chinouriri.d: British indie-pop riser with diaristic heartbreak anthems like 'All I Ever Asked'.
rachel-chinouriri.bio: South London indie-pop storyteller behind What a Devastating Turn of Events and 2025's Little House EP; a BRIT-nominated breakout who supported Sabrina Carpenter and brings warm, cathartic anthems live.

news.t: Rachel Chinouriri on new music and finding happiness
Chinouriri announces first North American tour

mother-mother.d: Canadian cult quintet whose quirky harmonies conquered the internet ('Hayloft').
mother-mother.bio: Canadian art-rockers celebrating 20 years and riding a TikTok-fueled second life; tenth album Nostalgia (June 2025) pushes into melodic rock and glam-pop, and the trio harmonies hit even harder live.

news.t: Mother Mother announce Nostalgia album and tour

smino.d: St. Louis' silkiest rapper-singer; funk-soaked flows and featherlight falsetto.
smino.bio: St. Louis's smoothest rapper-singer toured 2025's Kountry Kousins run with a seven-piece band and horns behind Maybe in Nirvana, his mellow, gospel-tinged album featuring Ravyn Lenae and Thundercat.

news.t: Smino announces 31-date Kountry Kousins tour

hot-mulligan.d: Michigan emo loudmouths; scream-along catharsis and absurd song titles.
hot-mulligan.bio: Michigan's self-proclaimed '#1 Hot New Band' and post-emo inventors, touring 2025's The Sound A Body Makes When It's Still. Cathartic screams, absurd song titles, and crowds that yell every word back.

news.t: Hot Mulligan announce new album and fall 2025 tour

the-dare.d: NYC's suited sleaze-pop provocateur ('Girls'); a sweaty 1:30 a.m. dance riot.
the-dare.bio: NYC indie-sleaze ringleader Harrison Patrick Smith — 'Girls,' Charli XCX's 'Guess' — kept 2025 rolling with the 'Afters' deluxe and 'freakquencies' dance EP. Suit, sunglasses, sweat: a filthy-fun dance party.

news.t: Clash: The Dare shares new single 'LCA'
The Dare gives ACL a taste of revamped indie sleaze

dora-jar.d: Whimsical alt-pop songwriter beloved by Billie Eilish; intricate, strange, charming.
dora-jar.bio: Shape-shifting alt-pop trickster touring debut 'No Way to Relax When You Are on Fire' plus 2026 singles 'Lucky' and 'The Explorer'. Billie Eilish-approved, whimsical and quietly virtuosic live.

news.t: Dreamy new single 'The Explorer' arrives
FLOOD: completing the puzzle of her debut

wednesday.d: Asheville's countrygaze heroes: pedal steel, shredding, and Karly Hartzman's gut-punch stories.
wednesday.bio: Asheville's countrygaze storytellers led by Karly Hartzman, with 'Bleeds' (Sept 2025) hailed by NPR as a rock-album-of-the-year contender. Pedal steel, blown-out fuzz and devastating Southern detail.

news.t: NPR: 'Bleeds' might be the rock album of the year
NME review: hard-won catharsis and thrilling noise

the-chats.d: Australian shed-rock larrikins; two-minute pub-punk ragers ('Smoko').
the-chats.bio: Sunshine Coast pub-punks who turned 'Smoko' into a global mullet anthem. Eamon Sandwith and co. play fast, dumb-smart and hilarious — 90 seconds a song, zero pretension, maximum shed-rock chaos.

news.t: Rolling Stone AU catches up with The Chats

bbno.d: Vancouver's goofiest hitmaker ('edamame'); irony-laced bangers, maximum fun.
bbno.bio: Vancouver's meme-rap entrepreneur (say 'baby no money') dropped a self-titled album in Oct 2025 and sold out world-tour legs across three continents. Pure absurdist party energy live.

news.t: Self-titled LP is a non-stop party: review
World tour adds third sold-out leg

geese.d: Brooklyn's wildest young art-rock band; 'Getting Killed' made them critics' darlings.
geese.bio: Brooklyn's most thrilling young rock band. 2025's Getting Killed topped year-end lists with its gorgeous chaos, and frontman Cameron Winter's unhinged-preacher stage presence makes their live show unmissable.

news.t: Getting Killed: most creative indie rock LP of 2025
Getting Killed makes a statement as 2025's best rock

blood-orange.d: Dev Hynes' luxurious art-pop and R&B; lush, melancholy grooves.
blood-orange.bio: Dev Hynes returned in 2025 with 'Essex Honey', a grief-soaked, gorgeous meditation on his English hometown featuring Lorde and Caroline Polachek — his first album in six years, and a hushed live stunner.

news.t: 'Essex Honey' is a chill masterpiece: review
NME: stunning reflections on grief

lil-jon.d: YEAH! The king of crunk turns That Tent into a midnight party time machine.
lil-jon.bio: The king of crunk and eternal hype machine — 'Get Low,' 'Turn Down for What' and a thousand WHATs. He plays the Farm before joining Pitbull's 35-city I'm Back Tour in summer 2026. Pure party, guaranteed.

news.t: Pitbull announces I'm Back Tour with Lil Jon

jackie-hollander.d: Indie-dance producer and vocalist; hazy, hooky house cuts like "Addicted".
jackie-hollander.bio: Bay Area-raised tech house riser who learned to DJ at summer camp — now Billboard's Up-and-Coming Dance Artist with releases on Nervous and REALM, and a 2026 festival run spanning Bonnaroo, Lolla and Electric Forest.

news.t: Billboard's up-and-coming dance artist of the month
Get To Know: Jackie Hollander

daniel-allan.d: Rising producer blending indie vocals with glossy dance-pop drops.
daniel-allan.bio: LA-via-Louisville producer whose 2025 'Time Well Spent' EP (with Louis The Child, NJOMZA) channels his self-coined 'synth garage' sound — glossy UKG and future-bass hybrids from a sharp young writer.

news.t: 'Time Well Spent' leans into his younger self
Daniel Allan shares 'Duality' EP

aszewo.d: LA trio Łaszewo blends breezy indie vocals with house grooves; 'messy' is the vibe.
aszewo.bio: Santa Barbara electronic-pop trio (say 'La-zay-woah'): producers Matt Ehrlich and Justin De La Fuente with singer Keeva Bouley. Emotional storytelling meets euphoric drops; they sold out three NYC shows in one day.

news.t: Łaszewo on Same Same But Different 2025

notion.d: UK bass producer with garage-flavored heaters like 'Hooked'; millions of monthly listeners.
notion.bio: Bristol bassline/UKG don Rob Penny, whose remix of Chrystal's 'The Days' hit UK No. 4 and 500M+ streams, earning a Mixmag cover and a BRIT nod; his sets careen from garage to DnB at full tilt.

news.t: Mixmag cover: viral success won't change NOTION
UKF names Notion one to watch for 2026

adventure-club.d: Canadian melodic-dubstep veterans; emotional drops and throwback feels.
adventure-club.bio: Canadian melodic-dubstep OGs riding a nostalgia wave: a 2025 Throwback Tour, vault remixes via their Time Capsule series, and fresh cuts like the Flux Pavilion collab 'Goddess'. Crystalline drops, big feelings.

news.t: Adventure Club rewind with Throwback Tour
Flux Pavilion collab 'Goddess' arrives

sidepiece.d: Grammy-nominated duo (Party Favor x Nitti Gritti); 'On My Mind' groove machine.
sidepiece.bio: Grammy-nominated house duo of Party Favor and Nitti, still flipping dancefloors with 2025 singles like the Bobby Shmurda rework 'Cash Out' and Westend collab 'Take Your Places' — pure tech-house party fuel.

news.t: SIDEPIECE and Bobby Shmurda flip 'Cash Out'
SIDEPIECE and Westend drop 'Take Your Places'

cloonee.d: UK tech-house heavyweight and Hellbent label boss; relentless club heaters.
cloonee.bio: Sheffield tech-house star Dave Bissett — Beatport's April 2025 Artist of the Month and boss of Hellbent Records — known for marathon sets and cheeky, vocal-driven grooves built for a packed late-night tent.

news.t: DJ Mag cover: Cloonee hits the big time
Beatport Artist of the Month, April 2025

major-lazer.d: Diplo's dancehall-EDM crew; nonstop hits and beautiful chaos at 1:30 a.m.
major-lazer.bio: Diplo's dancehall-EDM juggernaut, now a foursome with new member America Foster, back with 2025 mixtape GYALGEBRA and fresh off the Milano Cortina Olympics closing ceremony. First Bonnaroo in nearly a decade.

news.t: Major Lazer add first female member America Foster
Major Lazer returns with mixtape GYALGEBRA

ganja-white-night.bio: Belgian duo behind the signature reggae-tinged 'wobble' sound. 2025's Sprouted album and their first tour in five years reignited the SubCarbon faithful; their Mr. Wobble visuals make every set a cartoon fever dream.

news.t: Ganja White Night announce first tour in 5 years
Duo sets release date for new album Sprouted

inzo-sunrise-set.d: 'Overthinker' producer INZO guides a psychedelic sunrise comedown.
inzo-sunrise-set.bio: Chicago-born melodic-bass producer behind the Alan Watts-sampling hit 'Overthinker' (50M+ streams). Fresh off his 38-date Mirrorverse Tour and a Red Rocks finale, his cosmic, philosophical bass suits a sunrise slot.

news.t: INZO announces 38-date Mirrorverse Tour for 2026
INZO returns to Red Rocks April 3, 2026

probcause.d: Chicago rapper-producer fusing hip-hop and bass; longtime GRiZ collaborator.
probcause.bio: Chicago rapper-turned-bass-producer Collin Grimm (longtime GRiZ collaborator) leaned into his DJ era with the HEATWAVE dubstep EP (Oct 2025), fusing hip-hop bars with heavyweight bass and visual art chops.

news.t: ProbCause talks DJ era and dubstep EP HEATWAVE
What has ProbCause been up to?

mary-droppinz.d: LA selector dropping gritty house and bass for the late-late crew.
mary-droppinz.bio: LA-based DJ/producer and genre-defying bass selector who dropped the wub-heavy 'Drop In' on Zeds Dead's Deadbeats in 2025 and is leaning into 140 BPM territory with immersive, intentional festival sets.

news.t: Mary Droppinz enters the next phase of her career
'Drop In' lands on Zeds Dead's Deadbeats label

richard-finger.d: Anonymous duo making 'unreasonably heavy' dubstep; a SoundCloud cult hit turned Red Rocks act.
richard-finger.bio: Anonymous masked duo making 'unreasonably heavy,' meme-laced dubstep since 2024; they've already debuted at Red Rocks and dropped the EAZY MF FINGER EP with EAZYBAKED days before the Farm.

news.t: How Richard Finger flipped off the dubstep formula
EAZYBAKED and Richard Finger drop EAZY MF FINGER

eazybaked.d: Anonymous experimental-bass duo; glitchy, face-melting weirdness at 4 a.m.
eazybaked.bio: Orlando experimental-bass duo — lifelong friends Eric Ray and Andrew Principe — behind 2025's 'Respawn' EP and 'The Antennae': wonky, glitched-out low end that made them Wakaan favorites.

news.t: 'Respawn' EP: a testament to love
Monumental year ends with 'The Antennae'

lumasi.d: Rising bass producer with deep, meditative wubs to close out the Where Stage.
lumasi.bio: Fast-rising experimental bass producer who went viral remixing songs for his girlfriend on TikTok. Capped a breakout 2025 with the psychedelic Sun & Moon EP and support runs for Zeds Dead and Mersiv.

news.t: Bass breeds hypnotism on Lumasi's Sun & Moon EP
Your next bass addiction starts with 'The Cube'

midnight-generation.d: Mexican disco-funk band led by Fernando Mares; viral 'Young Girl' grooves.
midnight-generation.bio: Latin electro-funk five-piece from Chihuahua, Mexico blending disco, synth-pop and French-touch grooves; 2025 album Tender Love and the viral 'Don't Wait Up For Me' carried them to US festival stages.

news.t: Tender Love: the Mexican electro-funk band's new LP

arcy-drive.d: Long Island 'attic rock' band; scrappy, feel-good indie anthems.
arcy-drive.bio: Long Island 'attic rock' four-piece whose 2025 debut 'The Pit' was produced by Angelo Petraglia (Kings of Leon). Barefoot, scrappy and built for festival singalongs — they tour it through spring 2026.

news.t: 'The Pit': sun-soaked indie rock debut
Arcy Drive: wonderfully unfiltered

tash-sultana.d: One-person band looping guitar, keys and sax into hypnotic psych-soul jams.
tash-sultana.bio: Melbourne's one-person loop-station marvel, back to solo roots with the 'Return to the Roots' EP (2025) and a world tour after selling out Red Rocks in minutes. Guitar, trumpet, beats — all built live in front of you.

news.t: Tash Sultana shares 'Hold On,' maps US summer tour
First Australian headline shows in six years announced

alabama-shakes.d: Brittany Howard's reunited Southern soul-rock powerhouse; 'Hold On' hits harder live.
alabama-shakes.bio: Brittany Howard and co. ended a near-decade hiatus with a 2025 reunion tour, 'Another Life' — their first single in ten years — and 2026 protest cut 'American Dream'. That volcanic voice, back at full power.

news.t: Alabama Shakes map out reunion tour
First new song in a decade: 'Another Life'

the-neighbourhood.d: Dark SoCal alt-rock, back from hiatus; 'Sweater Weather' under Saturday lights.
the-neighbourhood.bio: The 'Sweater Weather' crew ended a multi-year hiatus with '(((((ultraSOUND)))))' (Nov 2025), their first LP in five years, plus a 2026 deluxe and world tour. Moody California alt-pop built for golden hour.

news.t: Rolling Stone: first Neighbourhood album in five years
Deluxe '(((((ultraSOUND)))))+' lands ahead of world tour

r-f-s-du-sol.d: Australian live-electronic trio headlining Saturday; 'Innerbloom' on the farm at midnight.
r-f-s-du-sol.bio: The Grammy-winning Australian trio headline off the back of Inhale / Exhale and the highest-selling electronic tour of all time (750k tickets); expect a cathartic, meticulously produced after-dark set.

news.t: Inhale / Exhale tour sets new global record
RUFUS DU SOL announce biggest North American tour

steph-strings.d: Melbourne percussive-guitar virtuoso; sunny, jaw-dropping instrumental folk.
steph-strings.bio: Melbourne percussive-fingerstyle guitar phenom mixing coastal indie, blues and Celtic flourishes. Self-released debut 'Feel Alive' (Jan 2026) hit No. 2 on the ARIA chart mid first world tour.

news.t: Billboard: Steph Strings wows Brooklyn on world tour
Debut album 'Feel Alive' announced with 'Devil Woman'

mountain-grass-unit.d: Birmingham's young jamgrass pickers; also guesting in Kesha's SuperJam.
mountain-grass-unit.bio: Birmingham jamgrass four-piece and IBMA Momentum Band of the Year whose boundary-pushing picking sells out rooms nationwide; debut studio LP Appalachian Smoke (Vance Powell) lands August 2026 on Dualtone.

news.t: Surging bluegrass band announces early 2026 dates
MGU plots massive 'Runnin' From Trouble' tour

holly-humberstone.d: British songwriter of aching, intimate alt-pop confessionals.
holly-humberstone.bio: British songwriter whose sophomore album Cruel World (April 2026) trades whispery confession for bigger, bolder pop. She hits Bonnaroo mid Cruel World Tour alongside Coachella and Gov Ball stops.

news.t: Holly Humberstone announces album Cruel World
Holly Humberstone announces North American tour

amyl-and-the-sniffers.d: Amy Taylor's Aussie pub-punk wrecking crew; feral, hilarious, unmissable.
amyl-and-the-sniffers.bio: Amy Taylor's Melbourne pub-punks are touring the acclaimed 'Cartoon Darkness' after stadium runs with Foo Fighters and main-stage festival takeovers. One of the most feral, can't-look-away live acts in rock.

news.t: 'Cartoon Darkness' review: hope amid dystopia
Sniffers ignite 2025 headline tour

rainbow-kitten-surprise.d: Genre-blurring NC favorites; 'Cocaine Jesus' singalongs and a SuperJam cameo.
rainbow-kitten-surprise.bio: The North Carolina alt-rockers' fifth LP bones (Sept 2025, with Jay Joyce) clears the skeletons out with their most energetic songs in years; Ela Melo's octave-leaping vocals anchor a kinetic live show.

news.t: RKS get back to the bones of their sound
Album review: RKS break free on 'bones'

teddy-swims.d: The tattooed voice behind 'Lose Control'; one of pop-soul's biggest belters.
teddy-swims.bio: Georgia's tattooed soul powerhouse behind 'Lose Control,' touring 'I've Tried Everything But Therapy (Part 2)' (2025) with Giveon, Coco Jones and GloRilla features. That voice hits even harder in a field.

news.t: Variety reviews 'I've Tried Everything But Therapy Pt 2'

weird-al-yankovic-bigger-weirder-late-ni.d: The parody legend goes Bigger & Weirder for a one-of-a-kind late-night Rooview.
weird-al-yankovic-bigger-weirder-late-ni.bio: The parody king brings his Bigger & Weirder spectacle to the farm: two-plus hours of costume changes, video gags and an expanded band that packed Madison Square Garden in 2025. 'Fat' suit included.

news.t: Bigger & Weirder tour rocks MSG: review
Don't miss Al's expanded band on tour

sunami.d: Bay Area beatdown hardcore; a 12:45 p.m. mosh-call alarm clock.
sunami.bio: San Jose's 'Real Bay Shit' bruisers — started as a joke, now one of hardcore's biggest draws. 2025 brought the 'Coast to Coast' split with Pain of Truth; their pits are legendarily violent, so brace yourself.

news.t: No Echo: Sunami and Pain of Truth join forces on split

congress-the-band.d: Rising rock-and-soul outfit bringing warm, retro grooves to This Tent.
congress-the-band.bio: Charleston's fast-rising Southern rock-and-soul crew, born from backyard jams in 2023 — groove-heavy originals, blues-leaning guitar and live chemistry that's earned a rabid Southeast following.

news.t: Roo Artist of the Day: Congress the Band

waylon-wyatt.d: Arkansas teen songwriter gone viral; raw, heartfelt country in the Zach Bryan lineage.
waylon-wyatt.bio: Teenage Arkansas country songwriter who went from viral porch videos to Darkroom Records; debut album 'Dustpiles' lands July 2026, with the Wyatt Flores duet 'Didn't Forget' already a calling card.

news.t: Waylon Wyatt announces debut album 'Dustpiles'

the-runarounds.d: Wilmington band born on the Prime Video series—now a real-deal touring rock act.
the-runarounds.bio: Wilmington, NC rockers formed for the Outer Banks creators' Prime Video series — the show was axed after one season, but the real-life band plays on, touring and writing a debut album of earnest, hooky heartland rock.

news.t: Deadline: 'The Runarounds' canceled, band plays on

passion-pit.d: Michael Angelakos' euphoric synth-pop; 'Sleepyhead' nostalgia in This Tent.
passion-pit.bio: Michael Angelakos' synth-pop project is back in earnest: the Pretty Penny era and a 34-date 2026 summer tour (Bonnaroo included) revive the falsetto-fueled euphoria of 'Sleepyhead' and Manners.

news.t: Passion Pit announce 2026 summer tour

kesha-presents-superjam-the-alchemy-of-p.d: Kesha's all-star pop ritual: Chromeo, Grouplove, Margo Price, RKS, Wyatt Flores, Blondshell & more.
kesha-presents-superjam-the-alchemy-of-p.bio: Bonnaroo's legendary collab set, helmed by Kesha: 'SuperJam Esoterica: The Alchemy of Pop' with Margo Price, Chromeo, Grouplove, Rainbow Kitten Surprise, Blondshell, Wyatt Flores and surprise guests in This Tent.

news.t: Bonnaroo reveals Kesha-hosted SuperJam lineup
Kesha to lead Bonnaroo's all-star SuperJam

osees.d: John Dwyer's hyper-prolific garage-psych machine; two drummers, zero letup.
osees.bio: John Dwyer's shape-shifting garage-psych machine, 28 albums deep, dropped Abomination Revealed at Last (Aug 2025); the twin-drummer live assault remains one of the most ferocious shows in rock.

news.t: Osees announce ABOMINATION REVEALED AT LAST
Dwyer expands Osees universe with live LP and film

snow-strippers.d: Detroit duo's lo-fi electroclash; the internet's favorite after-dark act.
snow-strippers.bio: Detroit-bred electro duo Tatiana Schwaninger and Graham Perez channel Crystal Castles-style trashy euphoria; fresh off an RIAA gold plaque and the harder-edged Night Killaz Vol. 2, their cult is going overground.

news.t: Under Snow Strippers' spell at St. Andrew's Hall

the-stews.d: Southern garage rockers with beachy riffs and festival-honed live energy.
the-stews.bio: Southern garage-rock road dogs out of Auburn and Charleston, riding 2024's Wolfgang Zimmerman-produced LP 'Chicken Fight.' Big riffs, beach-bum energy and a tour schedule that basically never stops.

confidence-man.d: Aussie dance-pop absurdists; choreography, costumes, and pure camp euphoria.
confidence-man.bio: Australia's most fun band: deadpan electro-pop, synchronized choreography and zero shame. The rave-soaked '3AM (LA LA LA)' pushed them onto festival main stages — a guaranteed party from minute one.

news.t: The party never stops on '3AM (LA LA LA)'
2025 North American tour announced

trixie-mattel.d: Drag superstar spinning a high-camp party set—expect hits, gags, and glamour.
trixie-mattel.bio: Drag Race superstar turned legit club DJ: her Solid Pink Disco party hit Coachella's Do Lab and toured arenas-worth of fans in 2025. Expect disco edits, house anthems and relentless camp.

news.t: Billboard: Solid Pink Disco tour announced

wyatt-flores.d: Oklahoma red-dirt phenom; tearjerker anthems plus a SuperJam guest spot.
wyatt-flores.bio: Stillwater, Oklahoma red-dirt riser whose breakout keeps snowballing: 'Live at Cain's Ballroom' arrived Feb 2026 and studio album 'Scared of Heights' lands in July. Raw, confessional, huge with young country fans.

news.t: Wyatt Flores announces 'Scared of Heights' album
First live album captures sold-out Cain's Ballroom run

sg-lewis.d: UK producer of glossy disco-house; nonstop feel-good grooves.
sg-lewis.bio: UK producer chasing 'nostalgia for eras you never lived' on third album Anemoia (Sept 2025, his own Forever Days label) — early trance and Balearic house built for golden-hour festival sets.

news.t: SG Lewis announces 'Anemoia' and US tour
SG Lewis sets 'Anemoia' release date and tour

flipturn.d: Florida indie rockers; 'August' euphoria built for the evening slot.
flipturn.bio: Jacksonville indie rockers riding 2025's Burnout Days and its 2026 Reimagined companion. Known for explosive, festival-honed live sets that build from shimmering verses to cathartic full-band eruptions.

news.t: flipturn release Burnout Days (Reimagined)
flipturn extend Burnout Days headline tour

freddie-gibbs-the-alchemist.d: Gangsta Gibbs and Uncle Al run back Alfredo: coke-rap poetry over velvet loops.
freddie-gibbs-the-alchemist.bio: Gangsta Gibbs and hip-hop's most surgical producer, touring behind 2025's Alfredo 2. Expect 90 minutes of pure bars and beats — no backing-track crutches, just one of rap's great duos in lockstep.

news.t: Alfredo 2 review: a succulent sequel
Alfredo: The Tour distills two masterpieces live

nikita-the-wicked.d: Theatrical dark-bass riser; commanding vocals over wicked drops.
nikita-the-wicked.bio: San Francisco bass/trap producer playing dance music's self-styled villain; the BLINDED BY VANITY EP (Dec 2025, Helix) and a pro ballroom-dance past fuel one of new-school bass's most theatrical sets.

news.t: Nikita the Wicked releases BLINDED BY VANITY EP
'LOOK' with Emi Grace amplifies the bass

juelz.d: Vancouver-bred Sable Valley star; emotional trap anthems with hard-hitting drops.
juelz.bio: Vancouver's classically trained trap jokester, an RL Grime Sable Valley standout who surprise-dropped the Adrenochrome: Zero mixtape in 2025. Hard-hitting hybrid trap with hip-hop bounce and zero filler.

news.t: Juelz surprises with mixtape Adrenochrome: Zero

deathpact.d: Masked mystery project delivering sinister mid-tempo and surgical bass.
deathpact.bio: Masked, identity-unknown bass/techno enigma who toured 2025's 'FROM DARKNESS, COMES LIGHT' with ARG-style puzzles, lasers and sinister industrial sound design. A cult favorite for mystery hunters.

news.t: Deathpact announces 2025 headline tour
New single 'GATEKEEPER' teases next chapter

boys-noize.d: Berlin electro-techno icon; grimy warehouse energy all night.
boys-noize.bio: Berlin techno shapeshifter behind 2025's '+/-' LP and this spring's full collab album with Nine Inch Nails, 'Nine Inch Noize'. Expect relentless, acid-streaked club techno from a true hardware obsessive.

news.t: NIN x Boys Noize album 'Nine Inch Noize' drops
Fifth studio album '+/-' announced

sub-focus.d: UK D&B heavyweight; euphoric, high-velocity festival drum & bass.
sub-focus.bio: UK drum & bass titan Nick Douwma, fresh off 'Contact' (Nov 2025) — a space-themed 14-tracker with Grimes, Katy B and John Summit — and a history-making run as the first D&B artist to headline Red Rocks.

news.t: EDM.com: 'Contact' pushes drum & bass into the cosmos
Sub Focus on D&B's US rise and his new album

sara-landry.d: The high priestess of hard techno; pummeling industrial kicks.
sara-landry.bio: Hard techno's 'high priestess' released debut LP Spiritual Driveby (Oct 2025) on her own Hekate label, fusing 140-160 BPM kicks with spiritual chants; her 2026 includes an Ultra b2b with Amelie Lens.

news.t: Sara Landry talks 'Spiritual Driveby'
Inside the rise of Sara Landry

chase-status.d: UK D&B institution; 'Baddadan' chaos after midnight.
chase-status.bio: UK drum & bass institution riding a stunning chart resurgence — arena headliners at home, Coachella and EDC conquerors abroad. Their late-night Other set will be the weekend's most punishing wall of breaks.

news.t: Chase & Status headline All Points East
Inside their stunning chart resurgence

gorgon-city-sunrise-set.d: UK deep-house duo Gorgon City steering a 3-hour marathon from 2 a.m. to sunrise.
gorgon-city-sunrise-set.bio: North London house duo Kye Gibbon and Matt Robson-Scott, still riding 2024's club-focused Reverie and a massive 2025 US tour. Their vocal-led anthems ('Ready For Your Love') should hit different at sunrise.

steve-cory-are-dead.d: Mystery billing on the Where Stage—show up and find out who Steve and Cory are.
steve-cory-are-dead.bio: The mystery booking of the weekend: an evening Where Stage DJ set from a duo with almost no public footprint. Bonnaroo's tiny-font wild cards often become the farm's best inside joke — show up and find out.

costa.d: Viral flipper CØSTA, whose 'Bass Cannon' and Timbaland edits broke dubstep TikTok.
costa.bio: Holding down a post-midnight Saturday slot on the relocated Where Stage — Bonnaroo's dedicated after-hours bass zone — with a set built around world-infused bass and heavy late-night low end.

news.t: Bonnaroo 2026 Where Stage lineup breakdown

clozee.d: French world-bass voyager; lush global textures, also half of LSZEE.
clozee.bio: French 'world bass' queen Chloé Herry weaves global textures into cinematic drops, with two new albums reportedly in the works and her LSZEE duo with LSDREAM also on the bill. Her 2:45am set is a late-night ritual.

news.t: CloZee reveals two new albums in the works
LSZEE singles tease anticipated album

big-gigantic.d: Sax-and-drums livetronica vets playing twice: a woods sunrise set and a Sunday banger.
big-gigantic.bio: Boulder livetronica duo — live sax and drums over festival-sized electronic drops — touring 2025's 'Fluorescence'. They pull double duty at the Roo: a late-night Where Stage slot and a Sunday set at The Other.

news.t: New album announced with Jennifer Hartswick single
Fluorescence Tour 2025 announced

smoakland.d: Oakland bass duo; heavyweight wubs engineered for the woods.
smoakland.bio: Bay Area duo Anthony Battaglia and Harrison Hays craft nostalgic 'old school' dubstep with modern weight; 2025 brought a 360-degree headline show in Denver and the Super Bass Bros tour with Dirt Monkey.

news.t: Smoakland bring genre-bending bass to Denver

effin.d: New Jersey dubstep riser with filthy, technical sound design.
effin.bio: Nashville bass innovator who fuses vintage samples with modern dubstep punch. Fresh off his 2025 sophomore album Daydreams and a Lost Lands resume, his sets are pure wobble-heavy energy.

news.t: Effin drops third album single 'Dreams Come True'
Effin continues hot streak with 'Bringing Me Down'

aly-aj.d: Disney-era icons turned synth-pop sophisticates; 'Potential Breakup Song' forever.
aly-aj.bio: The sisters' 2025 album 'Silver Deliverer' trades synth-pop for sun-baked Laurel Canyon folk-rock, cut live to analog in Topanga. Sets blend the new era with 'Potential Breakup Song' catharsis.

news.t: 'Silver Deliverer' album review
Live in SF: past and present ignite

trombone-shorty.d: New Orleans brass dynamo; a horn-fueled Sunday funk celebration.
trombone-shorty.bio: New Orleans' brass-funk ambassador Troy Andrews, touring Grammy-nominated 'Second Line Sunday' (2025) — a tribute to his mother released 20 years to the day after Katrina. With Orleans Avenue, it's a nonstop parade.

news.t: NPR: Trombone Shorty's hometown tribute album
Third annual Shorty Gras tour announced for 2026

tedeschi-trucks-band.d: Susan and Derek's 12-piece blues-rock revue; the farm's finest musicianship.
tedeschi-trucks-band.bio: Derek Trucks and Susan Tedeschi's 12-piece blues-rock institution hits the farm behind sixth LP 'Future Soul' (March 2026, Fantasy) — slide-guitar transcendence and one of the best live bands in America, full stop.

news.t: TTB announce 'Future Soul' album and 2026 tour
35+ Future Soul tour dates set behind new album

role-model.d: Tucker Pillsbury's heartthrob pop; 'Sally, When the Wine Runs Out' dance party.
role-model.bio: Tucker Pillsbury's heartbreak-pop project went supernova in 2025: 'Sally, When the Wine Runs Out' charted, he hit SNL, and his nightly celebrity-guest 'Sally' dance became the tour moment everyone films.

news.t: Role Model on TikTok fame and viral Sally moments
Why the Kansas Anymore era deserved a long goodbye

noah-kahan.d: Vermont's stick-season superstar closes the fest with shout-along folk-pop catharsis.
noah-kahan.bio: The Vermont folk-pop star closes Bonnaroo as Sunday headliner — a full-circle return after his 2023 That Tent set. Expect Stick Season-era singalongs and the weekend's biggest crowd-scream moments.

news.t: Skrillex, Strokes, Noah Kahan headline Bonnaroo
10 Bonnaroo 2026 storylines, from Kesha to Kahan

little-stranger.d: Charleston alt-hip-hop duo; breezy, funny beach-rap grooves.
little-stranger.bio: Charleston-based alt-hip-hop duo Kevin and John Shields — Gorillaz-meets-Beck melodic rap with a goofy streak. Fresh off festival runs (Gov Ball, Electric Forest) and their Broken Hearted Boys Tour.

news.t: A decade of evolution with Little Stranger

spacey-jane.d: Perth indie rockers; 'Booster Seat' melancholy wrapped in jangly hooks.
spacey-jane.bio: Fremantle, Australia's indie-rock heart-tuggers, touring third LP 'If That Makes Sense' (2025) — a shoegazy, Mike Crossey-produced step up. Expect mass singalongs on 'Booster Seat' and 'All the Noise'.

news.t: Rolling Stone AU reviews 'If That Makes Sense'
Melodic Mag on the band's ambitious third album

japanese-breakfast.d: Michelle Zauner's shimmering art-pop; joy, grief, and one triumphant gong.
japanese-breakfast.bio: Michelle Zauner's indie-pop project, touring 2025's moodier For Melancholy Brunettes (& Sad Women). Onstage she emerges from a giant pink clamshell — intimate sad-girl catharsis with arena-grade staging.

news.t: For Melancholy Brunettes review: beauty in sadness
Live review: from melancholy to euphoria

clipse.d: Pusha T and Malice, reunited and razor-sharp; precision luxury rap, brick by brick.
clipse.bio: Pusha T and Malice's 2025 reunion 'Let God Sort Em Out' (produced by Pharrell) earned five Grammy nods including Album of the Year — icy coke-rap precision, delivered live with commanding minimalism.

news.t: Clipse earn five Grammy nominations

kesha.d: Pop's glitter-punk survivor with wall-to-wall hits, fresh off Saturday's SuperJam.
kesha.bio: Pop survivor in full freedom era: 2025's Period was her first album on her own Kesha Records, and her 2026 Freedom Tour celebrates liberation and radical joy. She also curates this year's SuperJam.

news.t: Kesha announces The Freedom Tour
Kesha to lead Bonnaroo's all-star SuperJam

nat-myers.d: Korean-American blues howler reviving 1930s bottleneck stomp.
nat-myers.bio: Kentucky-born Korean American poet-bluesman playing slide-driven country blues in a century-old style (2023's Dan Auerbach-produced Yellow Peril); he's kept writing and performing while battling a rare cancer.

news.t: PBS: Nat Myers faces battle with a rare cancer

buffalo-traffic-jam.d: Bozeman, Montana folk duo gone supernova; intimate harmonies, sold-out tours.
buffalo-traffic-jam.bio: Bozeman, Montana folk duo Frankie Cassidy and Nathan Ross went from dorm-room jams to Arista Records; breakout single 'Fool's Gold' cracked Billboard's AAA top 5 in March 2026. Warm harmonies, big feelings.

news.t: Billboard Chartbreaker: 'Fool's Gold' duo
2026 kicks off with 'I Don't Care'

hemlocke-springs.d: The 'girlfriend' viral star; giddy, off-kilter 80s synth-pop with zero posing.
hemlocke-springs.bio: Naomi McPherson-approved bedroom-pop oddball Isimeme Udu, touring debut album The Apple Tree Under The Sea around Bonnaroo and Gov Ball. Quirky 80s-tinged synth-pop with an endearingly unfiltered stage presence.

news.t: hemlocke springs announces spring tour

fcukers.d: NYC indie-dance trio; 'Bon Bon' turned every club into their house party.
fcukers.bio: NYC dance-rock duo who became post-Brat scene darlings with 2024's Libera-winning Baggy$$ EP, then dropped debut album Ö in March 2026. Live, it's sweaty house-meets-indie chaos built for packed rooms.

news.t: Fcukers on the debut album they weren't sure they'd make

turnover.d: Virginia Beach emo-turned-dream-pop romantics; 'Peripheral Vision' favorites.
turnover.bio: Virginia Beach's shapeshifters — emo to dream pop to disco-funk — return with 'Down On Earth' (May 2026), their first album in four years and first self-released after a decade on Run For Cover.

news.t: Turnover announce 'Down On Earth,' share two songs
Band goes independent, returns to DIY roots

modest-mouse.d: Isaac Brock's indie-rock institution; 'Float On' plus decades of jagged classics.
modest-mouse.bio: Isaac Brock's indie-rock institution arrives fresh off An Eraser and a Maze (June 2026) — their first album in five years and first fully independent release since 1997 — with a fall headline tour to follow.

news.t: Rolling Stone reviews 'An Eraser and a Maze'
Modest Mouse announce first album in five years

soupless.d: Nashville roommate duo making fuzzy, pretty-guitar indie pop; an early-bird gem.
soupless.bio: Nashville alt-rock duo of two former Belmont University roommates making chill, eclectic guitar pop; debut album The Outside and singles like 'Signs' make them one of the lineup's small-font discoveries.

girl-tones.d: Bowling Green sister duo; classically trained, volcano-loud garage rock.
girl-tones.bio: Kentucky-bred sister duo (guitar + drums) signed by Cage The Elephant's Brad Shultz, now building toward a debut album after touring with Cage worldwide. Raw, high-energy garage rock from two classically trained sibs.

news.t: Sister duo Girl Tones release 'Cherry Picker'
Girl Tones share rebellious new single 'Got It'

blondshell.d: Sabrina Teitelbaum's 90s-leaning alt-rock; razor lyrics, huge choruses, SuperJam guest.
blondshell.bio: Sabrina Teitelbaum's '90s-leaning alt-rock got rawer and more assured on 2025's 'If You Asked for a Picture' — Rolling Stone called it sharp and secret-sharing. Cathartic, grunge-tinged and huge live.

news.t: RS review: sharp, secret-sharing rock
NME on 'If You Asked For A Picture'

audrey-hobert.d: The 'Sue Me' singer (and Gracie Abrams co-writer) stepping into her own spotlight.
audrey-hobert.bio: Gracie Abrams' best friend and 'That's So True' co-writer stepped out with 2025's wry debut 'Who's the Clown?' — conversational, extremely-online pop that made Pitchfork's best pop albums of the year.

news.t: Audrey Hobert is the star of her show
Wildly funny on debut 'Who's the Clown?'

del-water-gap.d: S. Holden Jaffe's heart-on-sleeve indie pop; also moonlighting in the SuperJam.
del-water-gap.bio: S. Holden Jaffe's heart-on-sleeve indie pop hit a new gear with Nov 2025's 'Chasing the Chimera' and a world tour he called the best of his life. A swooning, sweat-soaked romantic of the festival circuit.

news.t: Chimera tour turns Chicago into grandeur
Chasing chimeras and dreams in Houston

mariah-the-scientist.d: Atlanta R&B romantic; slow-burning, starry-eyed ballads like 'Burning Blue'.
mariah-the-scientist.bio: Atlanta R&B star whose 2025 album Hearts Sold Separately hit No. 1 on Billboard's R&B chart behind 'Burning Blue.' She arrives mid world tour with her dreamy, slow-burning love songs and effortless cool.

news.t: Mariah announces Hearts Sold Separately Tour
Hearts Sold Separately debuts at No. 1 R&B

motifv.d: Dreamy, starlit melodic bass from producer Motifv.
motifv.bio: Denver electro-soul/bass producer Joseph Kechter — Pretty Lights' younger brother — stitches gospel and soul samples into futuristic low-end design; the Where the Sun Sets / Behind the Moon EPs are his latest chapter.

news.t: Bonnaroo artist of the day: Motifv

a-hundred-drums.d: Gabrielle Watson's tribal, meditative deep-bass shamanism.
a-hundred-drums.bio: Denver-based producer Gabrielle Watson fuses tribal percussion, dubstep and psytrance into deep, hypnotic bass. Her 2025 'Fearless' EP landed on Subtronics' Cyclops Recordings.

news.t: On rebuilding herself and new music
'Fearless' EP: three unmistakable hits

san-holo.d: Dutch future-bass auteur of 'Light'; guitar-laced euphoria via his bitbird label.
san-holo.bio: Dutch producer-guitarist whose fourth album TRUE LOVE IN A MADE UP WORLD (June 2026) pivots to self-coined 'wholesome riddim' — heavy bass drops wrapped in heart-on-sleeve melody — with all-new live production.

news.t: San Holo's kaleidoscopic TRUE LOVE IN A MADE UP WORLD
San Holo releases album, announces autumn tour

daily-bread.d: Atlanta's electro-soul beatsmith; dusty soul samples over fat bass.
daily-bread.bio: Atlanta electro-soul producer Rhett Whatley flips dusty vinyl soul and hip-hop into head-nodding bass, fresh off his 27-date Flash Flood Tour and the 25-track 'Flash Flood II' mixtape.

news.t: 'Flash Flood II': cosmic electro-soul mixtape
How vinyl and electro soul fueled his rise

lszee.d: CloZee x LSDREAM joint project; double the cosmic bass dreamscape.
lszee.bio: The bass world's favorite team-up: LSDREAM and CloZee fusing cosmic wubs with world-bass textures. Their self-titled debut LP and 2025 doubleheader tour (plus Red Rocks runs) made this project an instant festival legend.

news.t: LSZEE tour captured beautiful snapshots in time
LSZEE headline tour features 15 doubleheaders
